definition: DHS/CISA cybersecurity content: ransomware, phishing, Shields Up, election-infrastructure security, MFA tips, Cyber Safety Review Board.
rationale: Hundreds of DHSgov tweets are pure CISA cybersecurity messaging mis-default-tagged as immigration; needs its own topic.

definition: FEMA / disaster & emergency management: hurricanes, wildfires, floods, tornadoes, disaster declarations, Ready.gov preparedness, disaster assistance applications.
rationale: Large volume of DHSgov FEMA/hurricane/Ready.gov content currently mislabeled immigration.

definition: Counterterrorism / homeland-security threat awareness: See Something Say Something, terrorism-related suspicious activity, threat briefings, NSSE security.
rationale: DHS counterterrorism awareness campaigns are a distinct mission area, not immigration.

definition: TSA aviation/transportation security: PreCheck, travel tips, checkpoint procedures, REAL ID, explosives detection K9s, pipeline security directives.
Diplomacy / foreign affairs: state visits, bilateral summits, ceasefires, international agreements, foreign-leader meetings (e.g. Trump-Xi Beijing, Russia-Ukraine ceasefire).
rationale: RapidResponse47/POTUS/WhiteHouse foreign-policy event coverage mislabeled immigration.
TSA is a DHS component frequently @-mentioned but not in the enforcement-agency gazetteer; add for find-tweets-about-TSA.

definition: Tweet mentions @FEMA / @fema (Federal Emergency Management Agency) handle.
rationale: FEMA is a DHS component frequently @-mentioned; supports disaster-response filtering.

definition: Tweet mentions @CISAgov (Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency) handle.
rationale: CISA already appears as an agency tag occasionally; ensure consistent emission for cybersecurity filtering.

definition: Post features a working dog (TSA/CBP/Border Patrol K9) as the subject (#DogsOnDuty series, canine handlers).
rationale: Recurring #DogsOnDuty content; cross-cuts TSA (aviation) and CBP (border) so a subject tag is the right home.

definition: Memorial / fallen-officer framing for an agent or officer killed or who died in service (distinct from theme:martyrdom which frames victims as the WHY for policy).
rationale: Agency memorial posts (e.g. CBP Marine Interdiction Agent death) need a memorial frame separate from martyrdom-of-crime-victims.

definition: Post features a naturalization ceremony, oath of allegiance, or newly naturalized U.S. citizens (USCIS citizenship milestone).
rationale: USCIS new-citizens/naturalization-ceremony content is clearly immigration but has no dedicated subject tag; recurring USCIS theme.
